reasoning: Based on the reference class of civil unrest/protests in African nations, the US State Department typically issues Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) advisories, reserving Level 4 (Do Not Travel) for nationwide breakdowns in security, wars, or full embassy evacuations. While Mozambique experienced severe post-election protests in late 2024, it is highly unlikely the entire country received a Level 4 advisory, as commercial flights and core government functions continued. Furthermore, if it didn't escalate to a Level 4 advisory during the peak of the crisis (e.g., November 2024 or inauguration in early 2025), the chance of it triggering a Level 4 in the brief window before July 2025 is very low. Thus, I estimate a 15% probability.
